policy_files.content:
id: canon:architecture_rules
kind: rule_set
version: 1

description: >
  Core architectural invariants governing the structure and technology
  choices of the SAGE system.

rules:

  - id: language_primary
    domain: architecture
    component: language
    description: The primary implementation language must be Go.
    effect: deny
    condition:
      field: project.primary_language
      operator: not_equals
      value: go

  - id: service_modularity
    domain: architecture
    component: services
    description: Services must be modular and isolated by responsibility.
    effect: deny
    condition:
      field: services.structure
      operator: equals
      value: monolith

  - id: api_boundary_required
    domain: architecture
    component: services
    description: Services must expose explicit API boundaries.
    effect: warn
    condition:
      field: services.api_boundary
      operator: equals
      value: false

  - id: default_database_sqlite
    domain: architecture
    component: database
    description: SQLite is the default persistence layer.
    effect: warn
    condition:
      field: database.engine
      operator: not_equals
      value: sqlite

metadata:
  enforcement_stage: planning
  priority: high

# ARCHITECTURE_RULES

This document defines the highest-precedence invariants for SAGE.

These rules are binding.
Agents must refuse when these rules are violated or when compliance is ambiguous.

This document governs governance. It does not govern downstream application architecture.

---

## 0) Supreme Authority

```yaml sagrule
id: ARCH-SUPREME-AUTHORITY
statement: "This file overrides all other docs and comments."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §0"
```

- This file overrides all other docs and comments.
- If ambiguity exists, prefer: [Isolation > Reliability > Resilience] (Placeholder).

---

## 1) System Boundaries

```yaml sagrule
id: ARCH-SYSTEM-BOUNDARIES
statement: "Agents MUST strictly adhere to system boundaries: canon/ and .docs/ for governance, agents/Jules/ for agent OS, .jtasks/ for planning."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §1"
```

- `canon/` and `.docs/` only when explicitly updating governance/canon.
- `agents/Jules/` for agent governance and skills.
- `.jtasks/` for planning and execution workspaces.

---

## Rule A1 — Canon Precedence Is Mandatory

```yaml sagrule
id: A1
statement: "Canonical precedence order must be followed as declared in ARCHITECTURE_INDEX.md."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A1"
```

Canonical precedence order must be followed as declared in `ARCHITECTURE_INDEX.md`.

Agents must not reinterpret precedence or “merge” conflicting rules.

If precedence does not resolve a conflict, the agent must halt and emit:

[AWAIT_HUMAN_VALIDATION]

---

## Rule A2 — One Task Group per Execution

```yaml sagrule
id: A2
statement: "Every request must be classified into exactly one task group."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A2"
```

Every request must be classified into exactly one task group.

Mixed task groups are forbidden.

If classification is ambiguous, execution must halt and require human validation.

---

## Rule A3 — Skills Are Procedural Only

```yaml sagrule
id: A3
statement: "Skills may execute only deterministic procedures and MUST NOT introduce architecture or modify canon."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A3"
```

Skills may execute only deterministic procedures.

Skills must not:
- Introduce architecture
- Modify canon
- Modify invariants
- Fill missing security boundaries
- Interpret ambiguous canon

If a skill requires design judgment, it must stop.

---

## Rule A4 — Spec-First for Non-Trivial Work

```yaml sagrule
id: A4
statement: "Non-trivial work requires spec artifacts (requirements, design, tasks) and explicit human approval."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A4"
```

Non-trivial work requires spec artifacts:
- requirements.md
- design.md
- tasks.md

Execution begins only after explicit human instruction.

If a request is non-trivial and no spec exists, the agent must halt and require spec generation.

---

## Rule A5 — Refusal Is Enforced

```yaml sagrule
id: A5
statement: "Refusal is required when canon conflicts exist, task groups are ambiguous, or security model lacks evidence."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A5"
```

Refusal is required when:
- Canon conflicts exist
- Task group classification is ambiguous
- Protected zones would be modified without mutation
- Security model is undefined or lacks evidence
- Determinism cannot be guaranteed
- Compiler validation fails (if enabled)

Refusal must be explicit and must stop execution.

---

## Rule A6 — Protected Zones Are Immutable Without Mutation

```yaml sagrule
id: A6
statement: "Protected zones (governance, canon) cannot be modified without formal Canon Mutation."
operator: MUST_NOT
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A6"
```

Protected zones define structural invariants and security boundaries.

Protected zones cannot be modified by:
- Skills
- Implementation tasks
- Routine documentation updates

Any modification requires Canon Mutation and human validation.

---

## Rule A7 — Mutation Is the Only Legal Canon Change

```yaml sagrule
id: A7
statement: "Canon may only change through formal mutation process involving human validation."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A7"
```

Canon may only change through formal mutation.

Mutation must include:
- Proposal
- Impact analysis
- Human validation
- Version traceability

Any attempt to “just update the docs” that modifies authority is a violation.

---

## Rule A8 — No Shadow Architecture

```yaml sagrule
id: A8
statement: "Agents MUST NOT introduce structural patterns not authorized by canon."
operator: MUST_NOT
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A8"
```

Agents must not introduce structural patterns not authorized by canon.

If a pattern is needed:
- remove it, or
- initiate a canon mutation to authorize it

Silent adoption is prohibited.

---

## Rule A9 — Determinism Is Required

```yaml sagrule
id: A9
statement: "Outputs MUST converge structurally given the same canon version, spec, and inputs."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A9"
```

Given the same canon version, spec, and inputs, outputs must converge structurally.

If determinism cannot be guaranteed due to ambiguity or missing authority, execution must halt.

---

## Rule A10 — HITL Break-Glass Marker Is Mandatory

```yaml sagrule
id: A10
statement: "When execution pauses for validation, the agent MUST emit [AWAIT_HUMAN_VALIDATION] on its own line."
operator: MUST
context: all
severity: CRITICAL
enforcement: REFUSAL
source: ".docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md §Rule A10"
```

When execution must pause for validation, the agent must emit:

[AWAIT_HUMAN_VALIDATION]

The marker must appear on its own line.
After emitting it, the agent must not continue execution.

---

## Rule A11 — Governance State Machine Is Mandatory

Refer to `.docs/canon/rule-schema-v2.governance.yaml` for structured definition.

SAGE governance operates as a deterministic state machine with explicit states and transitions.

States include:
- Normal
- Deep Governance Mode
- Spec Mode
- Execution Mode
- HITL Pause

If a request attempts to combine outputs from multiple states (e.g., planning + execution), governance must halt.

State transitions must be explicit and must follow the transition rules documented in governance.

---

## Rule A12 — Deep Governance Mode

Refer to `.docs/canon/rule-schema-v2.governance.yaml` for structured definition.

Deep Governance Mode is a governance validation state used before planning or execution.

Deep Mode must:
- run up to 3 validation passes
- converge only when two consecutive passes find no new risks
- evaluate precedence, protected zones, security implications, task group, determinism, shadow patterns, and mutation necessity
- output only risks, citations, and a safety verdict

---

## Rule A13 — Mode Transition Invariants

Refer to `.docs/canon/rule-schema-v2.governance.yaml` for structured definition.

The following invariants are binding:

- Deep Governance Mode MUST NOT transition directly to Execution Mode.
- Spec Mode MUST NOT perform execution.
- Execution Mode MUST NOT start without an approved tasks.md.
- Any ambiguity in transition legality requires HITL.

task_groups:
  - id: architecture
    modes_allowed:
      - ENTER_DEEP_GOVERNANCE_MODE
    skills_allowed: false

  - id: development
    modes_allowed:
      - ENTER_SPEC_MODE
      - ENTER_EXEC_MODE
    skills_allowed: true

  - id: operations
    modes_allowed:
      - ENTER_SPEC_MODE
      - ENTER_EXEC_MODE
    skills_allowed: true

  - id: docs
    modes_allowed:
      - ENTER_SPEC_MODE
    skills_allowed: true

  - id: audit
    modes_allowed:
      - ENTER_DEEP_GOVERNANCE_MODE
    skills_allowed: true

id: canon:architecture_index
kind: index
version: 1

description: >
  Top-level architecture index defining the canonical architecture components
  and references for the SAGE system.

components:

  - id: architecture.language
    name: Primary Implementation Language
    description: Defines the primary programming language used in the system.

  - id: architecture.services
    name: Service Architecture
    description: Defines how services are structured and interact.

  - id: architecture.database
    name: Persistence Layer
    description: Defines database and storage architecture.

references:

  - canon:architecture_rules
  - canon:security_model
  - canon:task_groups

metadata:
  maintained_by: canon
  priority: high

# Sovereign Agent | Architectural Index (Precedence & Governance)

This document defines the **canonical order of precedence** for all documentation in this project.
It is the secondary authority for resolving conflicts, as defined in `agents/Jules/JULES.md`.

---

## 1. Functional Governance Zones

The system is organized into six functional zones within the `.docs/` directory:

- `.docs/canon/`: Supreme Laws, Rules, and Invariants.
- `.docs/governance/`: State Machine and Mode protocols.
- `.docs/policy/`: Telemetry, thresholds, and conformance.
- `.docs/reference/`: Supporting guides and handbooks.
- `.docs/jules/`: Agent mode instructions.
- `.docs/compiler/`: Validation schemas and specifications.

---

## 2. Absolute Precedence List

In the event of a conflict between documents, the document appearing **higher in this list** always wins. Agents MUST refuse any request that violates a higher-precedence rule.

### I. Supreme Canon (Zone: canon)
1. [.docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md](./ARCHITECTURE_RULES.md)
2. [.docs/canon/SYSTEM_AXIOMS.md](./SYSTEM_AXIOMS.md)
3. [.docs/canon/SECURITY_MODEL.md](./SECURITY_MODEL.md)
4. [.docs/canon/INVARIANT_MODEL.md](./INVARIANT_MODEL.md)
5. [.docs/canon/CONTRACT_MODEL.md](./CONTRACT_MODEL.md)
6. [.docs/canon/ARCHITECTURE_INDEX.md](./ARCHITECTURE_INDEX.md)
7. [.docs/canon/MUTATION_PROCESS.md](./MUTATION_PROCESS.md)
8. [.docs/canon/VERSION_LOCKING.md](./VERSION_LOCKING.md)
9. [.docs/canon/DECISIONS.md](./DECISIONS.md)
10. [.docs/canon/TERMINOLOGY.md](./TERMINOLOGY.md)
11. [.docs/canon/DOC_STYLE.md](./DOC_STYLE.md)
12. [.docs/canon/ENFORCEMENT_MATRIX.md](./ENFORCEMENT_MATRIX.md)
13. [.docs/canon/WDBASIC.md](./WDBASIC.md)

### II. State & Mode Governance (Zone: governance)
14. [.docs/governance/state-machine.md](../governance/state-machine.md)
15. [.docs/governance/deep-governance-mode.md](../governance/deep-governance-mode.md)

### III. Agent Operating Instructions (Zone: agents/Jules/)
16. [agents/Jules/JULES.md](../../agents/Jules/JULES.md)
17. [canon/task_groups.yaml](../../canon/task_groups.yaml)
18. [skills/SKILLS_INDEX.md](../../skills/SKILLS_INDEX.md)

### IV. Procedural Skills & Work Orders
19. [SKILL.md files](../../skills/)
20. [Spec files (in .jtasks)](../../.jtasks/)

---

## 3. Non-Canonical Convenience Files

The following files are provided for token efficiency or quick reference. They carry NO canonical authority and are subordinate to all documents listed above.

- [.docs/reference/MINI_CANON.md](../reference/MINI_CANON.md)
- [.docs/reference/SAGE_OVERVIEW.md](../reference/SAGE_OVERVIEW.md)

---

## 4. YAML Rule Schema (sagrule)

Canonical rules may be expressed in structured `yaml sagrule` blocks within these documents. These rules are machine-validated and take precedence as the most precise expression of the project's law. The `tools/run-conformance.sh` tool MUST be used to verify the consistency of these rules.

---

## 5. Summary

If a rule in a lower-precedence document contradicts a higher one, the lower rule is **void**.
The Agent MUST refuse any request that violates this hierarchy.
The agent must STOP if design judgment is required but not specified in canon.

version: 1

system: SAGE

architecture:
  governance:
    machine: canon/
    human: .docs/
  task_system:
    root: .jtasks/
    active_pointer: .jtasks/current_task.yaml
    scheduler: .jtasks/scheduler.yaml
    event_log: .jtasks/events.log
  task_structure:
    iso_run:
      specs: specs/
      runtime_state: runtime/state/
      gap_report: runtime/GAP_REPORT.md
  agent_interface:
    entry_layer: .jules/
  context_engine:
    builder: context/context_builder.py
    index: canon/context/context_index.yaml

version: 1

strategy: dependency_first

rules:
  allow_parallel: false
  priority_order:
    - blocked
    - executing
    - ready
    - pending

selection:
  source: .jtasks/current_task.yaml

fallback:
  behavior: select_next_ready

task_id: "T-001"
status: not_started
current_step_id: ""
progress:
  completed_steps: []
working_set:
  allowed_paths: []
  forbidden_paths:
    - canon/
    - .docs/
    - .jtasks/_template/
decisions: []
last_update: "2026-03-04T21-00-00Z"
task_group: architecture

steps:

- id: S1
  title: "<step>"
  outputs:
  - "<artifact>"
    verification:
  - "<check>"

definition_of_done:

- id: D1
  description: "<condition>"

task_id: "<task-id>"

objective: "<goal>"

scope:
in:
- "<included>"
out:
- "<excluded>"

constraints:

- "<constraint>"

acceptance_criteria:

- id: AC1
  description: "<condition>"

overview: "<design summary>"

components:

- name: "<component>"
  change: "<add|modify|remove>"

interfaces:

- name: "<interface>"
  change: "<add|modify|remove>"

decisions:

- id: D1
  topic: "<topic>"
  choice: "<decision>"
  reason: "<why>"

risks:

- id: R1
  risk: "<risk>"
  mitigation: "<mitigation>"
